drift_candidates.element: Task row node named 'Card' (×11) vs canonical TaskCard
drift_candidates.note: PRIMARY DRIFT. Board task rows are flattened Cmp·Card trees: Card > Inner > [Status(18x18) + Col(TaskName, MetaRow(Tag, Pill[, Dep]))]. Canonical TaskCard is: TaskCard > [Touch44(44x44) > Cycle(18x18 box) , Body(Name, Meta(Tag, Win))]. Different node names (Inner/Col vs Body, Status vs Touch44>Cycle, Win vs Pill), missing Touch44 wrapper, extra Dep line. Verification must reconcile which component these rows actually are.

Canonical TaskCard wraps the 18x18 cycle box in a 44x44 'Touch44' frame (the tap-to-cycle hit area — the board's only interaction). Every board 'Status' box (×11) is a raw 18x18 frame with no 44px wrapper. Accessibility/interaction-spec gap to reconcile.

drift_candidates.element: Status-box fill glyphs diverge from StatusCycleButton
drift_candidates.note: Board DONE box = filled $success bg + child text '✓'; IN_PROGRESS box = #ff6b351f bg + primary '◼' (fontSize 9); empty box = border-only. Canonical StatusCycleButton has a single '◼' glyph (fontSize 8) and no fill-state variants; canonical TaskCard's Cycle has a child rectangle Fill (8x8) rather than a text glyph. Board encodes status via text glyphs, canonical via shape — reconcile glyph vs shape and the DONE-fill variant.

All 11 board 'Tag' frames have no cornerRadius (board radius token = 0). Canonical CategoryTag is cornerRadius 999 (pill). Either the global radius=0 token overrides canonical, or the canonical pill is wrong. Same applies to NavCard cliff chip vs board (chip kept 999). Inconsistent radius usage on this board.

drift_candidates.element: WindowStatePill rendered as bare frame, not a bordered pill

Board's pill also overloads three semantics through one shape: window-state (CLOSING/WINDOW CLOSED/NOT YET), blocked-status (BLOCKED + triangle-alert), and plain target date (no icon). CLOSING/CLOSED/BLOCKED/NOT-YET cards use a left-only 2px colored border (warning / muted-foreground / subtle) while DONE/OPEN cards use a full 1px $border. boardTriage.notes:
PRIORITY P0: this is the default Category view — one of the three core views and the canonical app shell (header + 280px sidebar + main + tabs). It anchors the window-state and status models and the shared component kit/tokens, all explicitly P0 per the product brief.

COMPLEXITY high (not very-high): the board is structurally a single screen (no side-by-side screen variants), but it densely lays out the FULL TaskCard state matrix — 3 status states (done/not-started/in-progress) × 4 window states (open/closing/closed/not-yet) plus a blocked overlay with a dependency caption, across 5 category groups and 11 task instances — on top of 7 distinct structural surfaces (AppHeader, Sidebar, NavCard, MissionDetailHeader, StatTile, ProgressBar, ViewTabs, CategoryGroupHeader). The high count of state permutations and the heavy, multi-axis drift (task rows mirror Cmp·Card not TaskCard; bare pills vs bordered WindowStatePill; sharp vs pill tags; missing 44px touch target) push it to high.

DRIFT SUMMARY: the single most important reconciliation is that the board's task rows are named 'Card' and follow the Cmp·Card node tree (Inner>Status+Col) rather than the canonical TaskCard tree (Touch44>Cycle + Body), and they overload one bare 'Pill' frame to express window-state, blocked-status, and plain target dates — whereas the canon separates WindowStatePill / StatusBadge / CategoryTag. Verification must decide canonical identity and unify the pill/tag/touch-target treatments. Also flag: VoiceFAB + Toast (the P0 voice command + confirmation flow) are NOT present on this board and must be specced from the dedicated voice board.

FAN-OUT rationale: 8 structural surfaces + a 12-permutation card matrix → too much for 1-2 agents; the TaskCard matrix alone is a full agent. 3 agents (shell/nav, header+groups, TaskCard+state-matrix) cleanly partition the work with minimal overlap.

drift_candidates.element: Timeline task row vs canonical TaskCard
drift_candidates.note: Canonical TaskCard is VERTICAL (Touch44>Cycle, then vertical Body of Name + horizontal Meta[Tag,Win]), padding 14, gap 12, name 15px. Board card is a HORIZONTAL row: [Status box | NameBlock | CatTag | Pill] with padding [11,14], gap 14, name 14px. Completely different node tree and layout axis. Highest-impact drift — affects all 10 instances.

Canonical TaskCard wraps the 18x18 Cycle in a 44x44 'Touch44' frame (a11y tap target). Board cards place the bare 18x18 'Status' box directly with no 44px hit area. Accessibility regression to reconcile.

drift_candidates.element: StatusCycleButton — status encoded by fill/stroke, not glyph
drift_candidates.note: Canonical StatusCycleButton = border box + disabled '◼' glyph (single canonical look). Board encodes status into the box itself: empty muted-subtle stroke (not-started/closed), border-hover stroke (not-yet), primary stroke + inner 8px primary square (in-progress), success fill + check icon (done). 4 bespoke status appearances vs 1 canonical.

Canonical CategoryTag = rounded tinted pill (fill #e05a9f1f), icon + category-COLORED label (fontWeight 600). Board CatTag = NO pill background, a separate colored ellipse dot + icon + MUTED-colored label (fontWeight normal). Different structure (extra dot node), different fill, different label color/weight. All 10 instances.

Canonical WindowStatePill is a window-state pill (OPEN, with optional leading dot/icon). Board's right-side 'Pill' carries MIXED meaning: window-state (WINDOW CLOSED, NO CLIFF, STARTS JUN 09, CLOSING·T-2D) AND task-status (DONE, BLOCKED). Stroke recolors by state (warning for closing, border otherwise). Verification must decide whether status and window-state are one pill or two components.

Board merges the window-state word and the countdown into one pill string ('CLOSING · T-2D'). Design brief treats window state and the days-until as distinct; canonical pills don't show combined strings. Token/format drift.

Board adds a second text line 'blocked by: Confirm ticket transfer' (mono 10px, muted-subtle) under the task name for blocked cards. No canonical TaskCard slot for a dependency hint line — net-new field to formalize.

drift_candidates.element: Dimmed/closed task via opacity 0.6 + muted fill

Canonical TaskCard has no opacity/dimmed variant; this dim treatment is a bespoke state to canonicalize (brief calls for muted grey + strikethrough on closed).

Canonical TimelineBucket = ref(TimelineWeekHeader) + Cards{ref(TaskCard) x2}. Board buckets inline a full bespoke 'BucketHeader' frame and bespoke card frames. Same intent, different tree; 6 instances.

drift_candidates.element: TimelineWeekHeader DaysPill — extra states + stroke recolor

Canonical MissionDetailHeader has Title + 'TARGET: <date>' + ref stat tiles + ref Progress, bordered, padding [24,32], title 26/700. Board block: title 25/600 WITH a leading live dot, a richer target LINE (label+date+separator+LOCATION), 4 flattened stat tiles, 3-seg flattened progress, NO border/padding wrapper. Significant structural + content (location, live dot) drift; also overlaps MissionSummaryCard.

Canonical Sidebar: fill $muted, MissionList of NavCard refs, header 'MISSIONS' 700/ls2, padding 18, 3 missions, meta '3/11 tasks'. Board Sidebar: fill $background, 4 bespoke mission frames with a left ACCENT RECTANGLE (not in canonical), header eyebrow 'MISSIONS' (normal weight) + a COUNT '04', per-row 74px fixed height, meta = window chip + 'N/N' (no 'tasks' word). Extra accent bar + count + 4th mission = drift.

Active mission chip ('1 CLOSING' with warning dot + warning stroke) and inactive chips ('T-12d' etc.) are bespoke mini-pills inside NavCards, distinct from both the canonical WindowStatePill and the brief's window-summary chip spec. Reconcile chip taxonomy.

split_plan.label: Agent B — Timeline buckets & task-row state matrix (the core view)

For TimelineWeekHeader/DaysPill spec all pill states (WINDOW CLOSED, THIS WEEK [primary], IN Nd, UNSCHEDULED) + range label + rule. For the bespoke horizontal TaskCard spec the FULL state matrix: status (not-started, in-progress=primary-square, done=success-check, blocked, not-yet/locked=STARTS, window-closed dimmed opacity 0.6), the merged window/status Pill (DONE/BLOCKED/CLOSING·T-2D/STARTS DATE/NO CLIFF/WINDOW CLOSED, stroke recolor), CategoryTag variants (medical/bureaucratic/travel/gear/tech with dot+icon+label), the blocked-by hint sub-line, and StatusCycleButton (note missing 44px touch target). Enumerate the ~10 card instances and tie each to a window-state × status combination. Capture all TaskCard/StatusCycleButton/CategoryTag/WindowStatePill/TimelineBucket drift candidates for downstream verification.

boardTriage.notes:
PRIORITY P0: Timeline is one of the three core MVP views called out in the brief; it is the canonical demonstration surface for the window-state model (too_late_by cliff -> WINDOW CLOSED / THIS WEEK / IN Nd / NO CLIFF) and the read-only status-cycle interaction. Non-negotiable for MVP.

COMPLEXITY high (not very-high): the file is 244 nodes/126KB but the structure is repetitive — 6 buckets of near-identical horizontal task rows. The real complexity is the STATE MATRIX, not novel surfaces: this single board exercises the entire window-state taxonomy (4 future/past states + no-cliff + closed-dimmed) crossed with all task statuses (not-started/in-progress/done/blocked/not-yet) and all 5 categories, PLUS a full page shell (chrome, sidebar w/ active+inactive missions, mission detail summary, view tabs). That breadth of states + the heavy, systematic drift from canonical components (the bespoke horizontal TaskCard, overloaded window/status Pill, dot+icon CategoryTag, missing 44px touch target) is what pushes it to high. Not very-high because there are no truly novel interactive organisms here (no voice/recorder/disambiguation flows live on this board).

FAN-OUT 2 agents: distinct-surface count is moderate (page shell cluster + the timeline view cluster) and they cleanly separate along a natural seam — Agent A owns the persistent chrome/mission-context that is shared with the Category/Dependencies boards, Agent B owns the Timeline-specific bucket+task-row state matrix and the bulk of the drift. One agent would overload on the task-row state matrix; three would over-fragment the shared shell. 2 is the right balance for a high-complexity, single-view board.

KEY DRIFT THEME for downstream bidirectional verification: this board's task row is a fundamentally different (horizontal) tree from the canonical (vertical) TaskCard, and it MERGES status + window-state into one pill while the canonical kit keeps StatusBadge / WindowStatePill / CategoryTag / StatusCycleButton as separate atoms. Reconciliation must decide the source of truth before build. Also note the brief explicitly mandates 'never overdue -> WINDOW CLOSED (muted grey + strikethrough)' and a 44px touch target — the board honors the wording (WINDOW CLOSED, opacity dim) but omits the 44px hit area and uses opacity rather than strikethrough.

surfaces.name: App shell / Top Header
surfaces.purpose: Persistent read-only top chrome (52px) shared across all desktop views: left wordmark OPS(orange)/BOARD(dim), right operator status block.

surfaces.name: Header operator/live block

surfaces.name: Mission Sidebar (280px)

surfaces.name: Mission NavCard — active
surfaces.purpose: Selected mission (AfrikaBurn 2026): card-elevated fill, 3px orange left-border, name + count 4/10, window-summary chip 'warning dot · 1 CLOSED · 2 CLOSING'.

surfaces.name: Mission NavCard — inactive (3 variants)
surfaces.purpose: Unselected missions with muted fill + border, each showing a different window-summary chip variant: Patagonia 'ON TRACK' (muted dot), Tokyo 'COMPLETE' (success dot), Passport '3 OPEN' (cat-bureaucratic dot).

surfaces.name: Mission Detail Header
surfaces.purpose: At-a-glance status for AfrikaBurn 2026: title (DM Sans 24/700), target line (flag icon + 'TARGET: 27 APR 2026 · 328 DAYS OUT'), 4-stat row, 3-segment progress bar.

surfaces.name: Detail stat tiles (DONE/BLOCKED/CLOSING/TOTAL)

surfaces.name: Mission progress bar (3-segment)

surfaces.name: Dependencies Content head
surfaces.purpose: Content section header: 'DEPENDENCY TREE' eyebrow + right-aligned legend 'corner-down-right icon + CRITICAL PATH' in primary/orange (the optional critical-path key).

surfaces.name: Dependency Tree (linked chain)
surfaces.purpose: Hand-rolled indented dependency tree of 8 task rows: roots (no connector), children (corner-down-right connector + depth indents). Demonstrates the full status x window-state x depth matrix.

surfaces.name: Tree row — DONE status
surfaces.purpose: Completed task (Pass fitness assessment / Research visa requirements): 18px square with success border + check icon, name in muted-foreground (the done-dim treatment), category tag only, no window pill.

surfaces.name: Tree row — IN-PROGRESS status
surfaces.purpose: Active task (Complete medical-team brief, Submit visa application, Book flights): 18px square primary border + 8px primary fill rectangle, name in foreground, category tag + window pill.

surfaces.name: Tree row — NOT-STARTED status

surfaces.name: Window-state pill variants
surfaces.purpose: Pill badges shown across rows: CLOSING·9D & CLOSING·4D (warning, timer icon), WINDOW CLOSED (destructive, lock icon), BLOCKED (muted, lock icon), OPEN (success, circle icon). Covers the window-state model's visible variants on this board.

surfaces.name: Unlinked group header
surfaces.purpose: Section divider after the tree: unlink icon + 'UNLINKED' eyebrow + '· 2 TASKS · NO DEPENDENCIES' count, separating tasks with no edges.

The 8 indented tree rows (Row · {task}) — the core organism of this board.

drift_candidates.element: Tree row Card vs canonical TaskCard
drift_candidates.note: The tree TaskCard wraps the StatusCycleButton + Name in a 'Left' frame and the cat-tag + pill in a 'Right' frame, with name nested under a 'Name Group' vertical frame (to host the optional 'Blocked By' reason line). Canonical TaskCard is unlikely to have this Left/Right + Name Group nesting — verify field/slot structure.

drift_candidates.element: Unlinked Card vs Tree-row Card (same TaskCard purpose, different tree)
drift_candidates.note: Unlinked cards put Name as a DIRECT child of 'Left' (no 'Name Group' wrapper) since they never carry a blocked-by reason. Two structurally divergent TaskCard shapes coexist on one board — must reconcile to a single component with an optional reason slot.

Blocked/not-yet rows REPLACE the 18px square cycle button with a bare triangle-alert lucide icon (warning). Canonical StatusCycleButton is a 3-state square that is 'always enabled' (design-brief §10) and blocked is advisory, never gating — so swapping the button out for an icon is a drift from the spec; the button should remain and blocked shown via pill/treatment only.

drift_candidates.element: StatusCycleButton done glyph uses lucide 'check' icon, not '✓' text
drift_candidates.note: Design-brief §10 specifies a ✓ glyph in near-black on success fill; board uses success border + success-colored check icon on transparent interior (no success fill behind it). Done fill/glyph treatment diverges.
Done rows render name in muted-foreground but with NO line-through. Design-brief §10/§9 require done/closed task names to be line-through. Strikethrough is missing across all done rows.

Detail-header BLOCKED stat value is $destructive. Window-model (§9, LOCKED #3) says blocked/closed is muted grey and red is danger-only. The stat tile color is a drift from the no-panic-red rule (though arguably an aggregate count, not a window-state).

drift_candidates.element: ProgressBar 'Active' segment is primary, not warning
drift_candidates.note: Design-brief §9 defines the 3-segment bar as success(done)+warning(closing)+destructive(blocked). Board renders success(done)+primary(active)+border(remaining) — different segment semantics/colors.

drift_candidates.element: Window pill — WINDOW CLOSED uses destructive + lock icon
drift_candidates.note: Design-brief §9 maps closed → muted-foreground + XCircle (faint) + 'WINDOW CLOSED' with card going bg-muted/strikethrough. Board uses $destructive stroke/text + 'lock' icon. Both color (red vs muted) and icon (lock vs XCircle) diverge, and the row card is NOT struck through/dimmed.

drift_candidates.element: Window pill — BLOCKED uses lock icon
drift_candidates.note: Design-brief §9 maps blocked → AlertTriangle. The pill uses 'lock' (which the brief reserves for not_before/not-yet); the row's status glyph separately uses triangle-alert. Icon assignment is inconsistent with the spec.

drift_candidates.element: CLOSING pill icon is 'timer', spec says 'Clock'
drift_candidates.note: Design-brief §8/§9 specify Clock for closing; board uses lucide 'timer'. Minor icon drift to reconcile.

Unlinked 'Pack dust-proof gear kit' shows an explicit OPEN pill (success, circle icon). Design-brief open-decision #4 leans toward 'absence = healthy' (show nothing for open). Board shows an explicit calm cue — divergence to settle. Also the other unlinked done card shows NO pill, so open-rendering is inconsistent even within this board.

On blocked/not-yet rows the Cat Tag dot+icon+label are rendered in $muted-foreground rather than the category hue (e.g. TRAVEL/BUREAUCRATIC go grey). Canonical CategoryTag is presumably always cat-colored; the dimming-when-blocked behavior is a board-specific treatment to capture as a variant.

drift_candidates.element: WindowStatePill aggregate variants in sidebar (ON TRACK / COMPLETE / 3 OPEN / N CLOSED·M CLOSING)
drift_candidates.note: Sidebar window-summary chips show aggregate strings not in the canonical per-task window-state set (open/closing/closed/not-yet). These are NavCard summary chips with bespoke copy — reconcile whether WindowStatePill supports aggregate/summary mode or a separate component is needed.

drift_candidates.element: Inactive ViewTabs carry stroke:$primary with no strokeWidth
drift_candidates.note: Inactive tabs (BY CATEGORY, TIMELINE) have 'stroke':'$primary' but no width/side (only the active DEPENDENCIES tab has bottom:2). Likely a leftover/artifact; canonical ViewTabs inactive state should have no primary stroke. Verify.

drift_candidates.element: Depth-2 indent done via fixed 36px 'Indent' spacer frame
drift_candidates.note: Depth is encoded as repeated 36px-wide empty 'Indent' frames prepended before the connector (depth 2 = 1 indent + connector; deeper = N indents). Canonical DependencyTreeNode likely parametrizes depth differently (depth × 36 padding). The flattened repeated-spacer approach is a drift to reconcile.

drift_candidates.element: Critical-path expressed only via connector color
drift_candidates.note: Critical path is shown by coloring the connector glyph $primary (vs muted-subtle) on Submit visa application + Collect passport rows, with a legend chip. There is no other critical-path marker on the cards themselves. Verify this matches the intended DependencyConnector critical-path variant.

drift_candidates.element: Card corners — no cornerRadius on cards (radius 0) but pills use 999
drift_candidates.note: Consistent with --radius:0 + rounded-full pills (LOCKED #2), but worth confirming the TaskCard/StatTile carry no stray radius; only ellipse dots and pills are rounded here.

split_plan.label: Shell + sidebar + detail header
split_plan.scope: Spec the shared desktop frame as it appears on this board: Top Header (wordmark + SyncStatus operator/live/date), Sidebar (MISSIONS head + 4 NavCards incl. active/inactive states and the 4 window-summary chip variants), MissionDetailHeader (title, target line, 4 StatTiles incl. the destructive-BLOCKED drift, 3-segment ProgressBar drift), and ViewTabs (active/inactive + the stray primary-stroke artifact). Component-level functional spec + drift notes for AppHeader, SyncStatus, Sidebar, NavCard, WindowStatePill(summary), StatTile, ProgressBar, ViewTabs.

split_plan.label: Dependency tree organism + node/connector/status
split_plan.scope: Spec the core Dependencies organism: the DependencyTreeNode row anatomy (Left/Right + Name Group nesting), depth/indent encoding (36px spacers), DependencyConnector normal-vs-critical-path variants + the CRITICAL PATH legend, StatusCycleButton tri-state + the blocked triangle-alert substitution drift, done glyph/strikethrough drifts, and cycle-safe/depth rendering behavior from design-brief §11 Dependencies + §10 status model. Enumerate all 8 row state combinations.

split_plan.label: Task card content: window-state, category, blocked-by + unlinked group
split_plan.scope: Spec the per-task content shared by tree rows and unlinked cards: TaskCard structure reconciliation (Name Group present vs absent), CategoryTag (5 hues + greyed-when-blocked variant), the full WindowStatePill set (CLOSING·Nd / WINDOW CLOSED / BLOCKED / OPEN incl. icon+color drifts vs §9), the 'NOT YET · blocked by: {name}' reason line, and the UnlinkedGroupHeader + 2 unlinked card variants (open-cue vs no-pill inconsistency). Anchor to window-state model §9 and flag every icon/color divergence.

boardTriage.notes:
PRIORITY P0: Dependencies is one of the three core MVP views (project brief + design-brief §11 + scaffolding S4 'port the 3 views 1:1'). The board also doubles as the canonical home of the full app shell (header/sidebar/detail-header/tabs) and the StatusCycleButton + window-state pill matrix, so it is squarely MVP-core.

COMPLEXITY high (not very-high): ~18 distinct surfaces/variants and 234 nodes, but much of it is repetition of two card archetypes (tree-row TaskCard, unlinked TaskCard) across a status x window-state x depth matrix. The genuinely novel/complex parts are the hand-rolled indented tree (depth spacers + connectors + critical-path coloring + cycle-safety) and a dense set of drift candidates against the window-state spec. It is more involved than a low/medium single-view board but does not hit very-high (no multi-screen flows, no voice/recording state machine, no modal/dialog surfaces).

FAN-OUT 3: driven by 3 natural cohesion clusters, not file size — (1) the shared shell/sidebar/detail chrome, (2) the dependency-tree organism (node/connector/status/critical-path), (3) the per-task content kit (TaskCard/CategoryTag/WindowStatePill/blocked-by + unlinked group). Each cluster owns a distinct slice of the 82-component set and a distinct band of drift candidates, so they parallelize cleanly with minimal overlap.

DRIFT EMPHASIS: the heaviest reconciliation load is in the window-state rendering — closed uses destructive+lock (spec: muted+XCircle, struck-through), blocked swaps the status button for an icon (spec: button always present/enabled, blocked advisory only), done lacks strikethrough, closing uses 'timer' not 'Clock', and OPEN is shown explicitly in one place but omitted in another. Two structurally different TaskCard trees (Name-Group-wrapped vs flat) coexist. The detail BLOCKED stat and the ProgressBar 'active' segment both use colors that diverge from §9. All flagged for the bidirectional verification pass.

surfaces.name: Voice FAB — Idle state
surfaces.purpose: The single voice input affordance at rest: 72px circular orange button with Mic glyph, 'TAP TO RECORD' hint. The entry point to the whole voice command pipeline.

surfaces.name: Voice FAB — Recording state
surfaces.purpose: Active capture: destructive-red 72px button with Square (stop) glyph inside a 96px destructive pulse ring, plus an inline 10-bar primary waveform and a mm:ss mono timer (00:07), '● TAP TO STOP' hint.

surfaces.name: Voice FAB — Processing state
surfaces.purpose: Transcription in flight: muted 72px button with bordered ring and orange Loader glyph, 'TRANSCRIBING…' hint.

surfaces.name: Voice FAB — Error state
surfaces.purpose: Capture/transcription failed: muted 72px button with destructive border + triangle-alert glyph, a destructive red underline bar, 'TAP TO RETRY' hint.

surfaces.name: Confirmation Toast — Success
surfaces.purpose: Reports a completed voice/MCP mutation. Card with 2px success top-accent, mono '✓ MARKED DONE' header, DM Sans task name body, 'AUTO-DISMISS · 4S' meta. Read-only result surface, auto-dismissing.

surfaces.name: Confirmation Toast — Needs Confirmation
surfaces.purpose: Gates a destructive/low-confidence intent behind a tap. Card with 2px warning top-accent, 'CONFIRM DELETE' header, body 'Delete task “Cardiology follow-up”?', and a two-button action row (destructive DELETE + outline CANCEL). Persistent (no auto-dismiss).

surfaces.name: Disambiguation Toast
surfaces.purpose: Multiple matches for a voice command. Card with 2px border-hover top-accent, 'WHICH TASK?' header, prompt body, and an inline list of 2 tap-to-pick rows (each: task name + window/category code MED / TECH). The lightweight inline picker variant (vs the full DisambiguationPicker panel).

surfaces.name: Command Error Toast
surfaces.purpose: A voice command could not be parsed/executed. Card with 2px destructive top-accent, 'COMMAND FAILED' header, body quoting the failed transcript, 'TAP TO DISMISS' meta. role=alert surface.

surfaces.name: Voice Query Result panel
surfaces.purpose: Answers a read-only voice question from computed window-state. 540px panel with a 3px orange left rail over card-elevated content: calendar-clock icon + 'CLOSING THIS WEEK' header (orange), a DM Sans answer line, and a 3-row mono task list (name + window code). Transient, info-tinted, NO actions — explicitly distinct from the warning confirmation toast.

surfaces.name: Gallery scaffold (header + 3 section labels)
surfaces.purpose: Non-product chrome: gallery title 'VOICE COMMAND & FEEDBACK' + subtitle, and three numbered section headers (01 · VOICE FAB, 02 · TOASTS, 03 · VOICE QUERY RESULT). Documentation framing only — not a shipping surface.

components_used.notes: Canonical VoiceFAB is a single idle-only def (96px wrap, orange Pulse Ring #ff6b3559, 72px button, 28px Mic). Board renders 4 bespoke state cells (idle/recording/processing/error) — none is a ref to the canonical; all are flattened, and the documented 'requesting' state is absent.

Not used as a component. The recording FAB cell flattens its own inline waveform (10 bars @3px wide, height 34) + 00:07 timer. Canonical RecordingPanel is a full card (status dot + RECORDING label + timer header, 40-bar waveform @4px). Functionally overlapping but structurally divergent.
Processing FAB cell uses a 30px 'loader' icon inside the button. Canonical Spinner is a row (20px 'loader-circle' + 'PROCESSING COMMAND…' label). Icon name and composition differ.

SUCCESS cell is an exact flatten of canonical Toast (same content/accent/meta). NEEDS-CONFIRMATION, DISAMBIGUATION, and ERROR cells are bespoke Toast variants — canonical Toast has NO actions row, NO inline picks; these add them.
Confirm-toast action row has DELETE (destructive fill) + CANCEL (outline) as hand-built frames, NOT refs to the canonical Button (6×5 CVA). Drift candidate.

The board's Disambiguation toast is a much lighter inline 2-pick variant. Canonical DisambiguationPicker is a full panel: git-fork warning header, confidence % per candidate, CategoryTag + StatusBadge refs, 'SAY IT AGAIN' retry. Board version omits all of that.

Board 'Query Panel' is a near-exact flatten of canonical VoiceQueryResultCard (same 540px, same orange 3px left rail, same calendar-clock header, same 3 rows / same copy) EXCEPT the board wraps it in an extra outer stroke ($border 1px) the canonical lacks.
Query rows and disambiguation picks show window info as plain mono text (MON–WED, TUE 17:00, MED, TECH) rather than the canonical WindowStatePill component. Plain-text drift.
Disambiguation picks reduce category to a bare 'MED'/'TECH' mono code, not a CategoryTag (icon+label+tint). Canonical DisambiguationPicker uses CategoryTag refs; board variant does not.
Recording cell uses '●' inside the hint text and a destructive ellipse pulse ring rather than a StatusDot. RecordingPanel canonical uses an 8px destructive Dot ellipse.

drift_candidates.element: Voice FAB multi-state set (4 cells, ids kcGtQ/SUKpb/uDTbs/KgVFG)
drift_candidates.note: Canonical VoiceFAB (F36g7L) is idle-only with a single tree. The board invents 4 distinct bespoke state trees with no shared component basis. Verification must decide whether VoiceFAB grows state variants or whether RecordingPanel/Spinner/ErrorStateCard own the non-idle states.

drift_candidates.element: Idle FAB Mic size 30px (VW9jB) vs canonical 28px (O4jXV)
drift_candidates.note: 2px icon-size mismatch between board idle FAB and canonical VoiceFAB Mic.

drift_candidates.element: Recording pulse ring fill (mxDl5: stroke $destructive, solid)
drift_candidates.note: Canonical VoiceFAB Pulse Ring is orange at ~35% alpha (#ff6b3559). Board recording ring is full-opacity destructive red. Design-brief says recording flips button to destructive but keeps an ORANGE waveform; ring color treatment needs reconciliation.

drift_candidates.element: Inline recording waveform (wUjxw): 10 bars, 3px wide, max h34
drift_candidates.note: Canonical RecordingPanel waveform (vbdqN) is ~40 bars at 4px wide, max h34, full-width. Board flattens a tiny 10-bar waveform beside the button instead of using RecordingPanel. Bar count, width, and container differ.
Canonical RecordingPanel Timer (Ad3pN) is 13px weight 600 in a space-between top row. Board timer is 18px stacked under the waveform — different size and placement.

drift_candidates.element: Processing loader icon (PAmqB): icon 'loader', 30px, fill $primary inside $muted button
drift_candidates.note: Canonical Spinner uses 'loader-circle' at 20px. Icon name differs (loader vs loader-circle); presentation (inside a 72px muted button vs inline row) differs.

drift_candidates.element: Error FAB cell (KgVFG → Sbai0): muted button + destructive border + triangle-alert + red underline bar (fDa9o)
drift_candidates.note: No canonical error-FAB exists. The 'Red Line' rectangle is a bespoke motif. Verification must map this to either a VoiceFAB error variant or ErrorStateCard.

Design-brief §11 lists 5 voice states (idle/requesting/recording/processing/error). Board renders only 4 — the 'Allow microphone…' requesting state is absent. Spec must add it; verification should flag the gap.

drift_candidates.element: Confirmation toast Actions row (XXjyT) with Btn DELETE (Wqo6m) + Btn CANCEL (DfyVw)
drift_candidates.note: Canonical Toast (kTqS3) has NO actions[] (brief §14 explicitly notes Toast needs an actions[] added). Buttons are hand-built frames, not Button refs. Both the Toast component and these buttons are drift to reconcile.

drift_candidates.element: Disambiguation toast inline Picks (P2wCZ) vs canonical DisambiguationPicker (cLP7O)
drift_candidates.note: Board uses a lightweight 2-row inline pick list inside a Toast (each: name + bare MED/TECH code). Canonical DisambiguationPicker is a standalone panel with confidence %, CategoryTag+StatusBadge refs, git-fork header, and a 'SAY IT AGAIN' retry. These are two materially different artifacts for the same intent — decide whether the toast-inline variant is a separate component or DisambiguationPicker has a compact mode.

Disambiguation toast top-accent uses $border-hover (neutral grey), unlike SUCCESS=$success, CONFIRM=$warning, ERROR=$destructive. No documented accent token for disambiguation in the brief; confirm this is intentional vs should be warning/info.

drift_candidates.element: Disambiguation pick Win codes 'MED'/'TECH' (p47JG/HpKsK) as plain mono text
drift_candidates.note: Not WindowStatePill nor CategoryTag — bare abbreviations. Ambiguous whether MED/TECH denote category (medical/tech) or a window code. Needs semantic clarification + redundant-channel (icon+label+color) per LOCKED #6.

Canonical Toast body text is $foreground (full). Both the ERROR and DISAMBIGUATION board bodies use $muted-foreground for body copy — a deliberate-looking dim, but a divergence from the canonical Toast body color.

drift_candidates.element: Voice Query Panel extra outer stroke wrapper (jfz3H)
drift_candidates.note: Board wraps the query card in an outer frame with fill $primary + stroke $border 1px; canonical VoiceQueryResultCard (YMkie) has the orange rail via 3px left padding but NO outer border stroke. Extra hairline border is drift.

drift_candidates.element: Query row name font = JetBrains Mono (rJaiz etc.)
drift_candidates.note: Matches canonical (mono 12px). Confirm: brief §6 says task NAMES are DM Sans; here query-result rows render names in mono. Both board and canonical agree on mono — flag as an intentional in-result styling exception to the global name-in-DM-Sans rule.

drift_candidates.element: Section gallery chrome (R664ri scaffold) is bespoke, non-component
drift_candidates.note: Gallery header/section labels use raw text nodes, not Eyebrow/Type tokens. Layout-only scaffolding; should not be shipped or mapped to components but must be excluded from the build.

split_plan.label: Voice capture states (FAB + recorder + spinner/error)
split_plan.scope: Spec the complete voice-input pipeline surface: VoiceFAB and all of its lifecycle states — idle, the MISSING 'requesting'/permission state (must be added), recording (button→destructive, Square glyph, pulse ring, inline waveform, mm:ss timer), processing (Loader), and error/retry. Define the canonical contract that reconciles VoiceFAB + RecordingPanel + Spinner + the bespoke error treatment, the orange-vs-red pulse/waveform color rules, icon names (loader vs loader-circle, mic 28/30), pointer/tap interaction model (push-to-talk vs tap-toggle), accessibility (role=alert on error, aria-hidden pulse), and the bottom-right mobile placement. Resolve drift items 1–8.

split_plan.label: Feedback surfaces (toasts, disambiguation, voice-query result)
split_plan.scope: Spec the four toast variants (success/auto-dismiss, needs-confirmation/persistent with the Confirm+Cancel action row, command-error/role=alert) plus the inline Disambiguation toast and the transient Voice Query Result panel. Define the Toast component's new actions[] API, the accent-token-per-variant map (success/warning/border-hover/destructive), the dwell/persistence rules (4s success vs persistent confirm vs ~8s query), the relationship between the lightweight inline disambiguation toast and the full DisambiguationPicker panel, window/category code semantics (MED/TECH, WindowStatePill/CategoryTag usage), and the visual distinction between info-query and warning-confirm. Resolve drift items 9–16.

boardTriage.notes: PRIORITY P0: This is MVP-core. The voice command + toast/confirmation flow is named in the product brief as the ONLY input affordance and 'the biggest net-new piece' (design-brief §11). Window-state copy ('CLOSING THIS WEEK', no 'overdue') and the read-only model both manifest here, so this board anchors the core interaction contract. COMPLEXITY high (not very-high): ~114 nodes / 58KB but the file is largely flat repetition (waveform bars, parallel toast cells). The real complexity is in DISTINCT surfaces — 9 product surfaces spanning 11 states across 3 unrelated component families (FAB lifecycle, toast variants, query panel), each with meaningful drift from canon and one DOCUMENTED-BUT-MISSING state ('requesting'). That breadth + drift density (16 candidates) pushes it above medium, but there is no deep nesting or novel layout system, so it is not very-high. FAN-OUT = 2: the board cleanly bisects into (a) voice-capture lifecycle and (b) feedback/result surfaces — two coherent, near-independent spec domains with their own component sets and interaction models; splitting finer would fragment the toast-family contract. KEY DRIFT THEME for downstream verification: nearly every surface here is a bespoke flatten, not a component ref (only the SUCCESS toast and the Query panel are near-faithful copies). The FAB states have no canonical multi-state basis at all, the confirmation/disambiguation toasts extend the canonical Toast beyond its current def, and the inline disambiguation toast competes with the standalone DisambiguationPicker — bidirectional verification must decide canonical ownership for each.

surfaces.name: Mobile App Header / SyncStatus

surfaces.name: Mission Summary card

Live dot (success ellipse 6px) + 'SYNCED' mono caps label. Matches SyncStatus purpose.
Name + TARGET + stats + progress in one $card block. On mobile this fuses MissionDetailHeader + MissionSummaryCard + StatTiles + ProgressBar into a single flattened card.
Purpose-overlaps Mission Summary (mission name + target + stats). Folded into the same flattened card here, not a separate header.
DONE / BLOCKED / CLOSING / TOTAL value-over-label pairs. Flattened: bare frame with two text nodes, no tile surface/border/padding — divergent from a canonical StatTile.
3-segment bar (done/closing/remaining). Matches the adapted 3-segment ProgressBar from brief §9/§14, but segment widths are hardcoded px (106/80) and no card-elevated track frame is explicit.
dot + cat label + spacer + count. Strong match. Uses an explicit 'spacer' frame (width fill, height 1) instead of justify-between.
8px category dots in group headers; 6px category dots in task meta rows. Two sizes used as plain ellipses.
Core repeated row across 5 groups. See drift candidates — structurally bespoke vs canonical TaskCard.

18px square 'Status' frame. 4 visual variants present (not-started empty, done success+check, blocked/not-yet muted+lock, closed border+x). MISSING the in-progress variant (border-primary + bg-primary/12 + filled square).

The 'Pill' frame (rounded-full, card-elevated fill, icon + mono caps label). Variants: CLOSING (warning+timer), OPEN (muted+timer), DONE (success+check), BLOCKED (muted+ban), WINDOW CLOSED (subtle+x). Drift: brief §9 specifies XCircle for closed and Clock for closing; board uses lucide 'x' and 'timer'.

The 'Cat' frame: 6px dot + lucide cat icon + cat label. Drift: this is NOT a /12-tinted Badge pill (per brief §6/§7 CategoryTag should be a bg-cat-x/12 badge); here it is a bare inline dot+icon+text with no pill background.
Overlaps WindowStatePill role; the pill doubles as the status/window badge. Counted once via WindowStatePill above.
56px circular primary FAB with mic + orange glow shadow. Only the idle state shown. Matches canonical VoiceFAB.
Mission Summary card + 13 task cards all use the $card surface with $card fill, padding, sharp corners.
Mono uppercase tracked labels (SYNCED, TARGET, group labels, stat labels) all match the Eyebrow / Label/Mono typographic role.
Task names + mission name in DM Sans map to Type/Body / Type/Subtitle roles.

drift_candidates.note: Flattened bespoke row, diverges from canonical TaskCard: card layout is HORIZONTAL (Status frame + Content column), card itself does NOT carry the brief §9 window-state treatment (no border-l-2 amber for closing, no bg-muted for closed) — only the closed/blocked cards use opacity (0.55/none) and the pill carries state. Brief says the card itself should take border/opacity/strikethrough.

Bespoke 18px square frame, no reusable flag. MISSING the 'in-progress' variant entirely (border-primary + bg-primary/12 + filled square per brief §10). Has FOUR visual treatments instead: not-started, done, not-yet(lock), closed(x) — the lock and x variants are window/derived states folded onto the status button, which the brief models as separate (status is 3-state; blocked/closed are derived).

Diverges from canonical CategoryTag/Badge: rendered as bare inline [6px dot + 12px lucide icon + mono caps label] with NO pill background. Brief §6/§7/§14 specify CategoryTag as a bg-cat-x/12 tinted Badge. Also DOUBLE category encoding within one card (dot+icon+label here, plus the group header already shows the category) — redundant on a single-category group view.

drift_candidates.element: Category icons mismatch the brief icon table
drift_candidates.note: Board uses lucide pill/file-text/route/package/cpu for medical/bureaucratic/travel/gear/tech. Brief §4 specifies Stethoscope/FileText/Plane/Backpack/Cpu. Only FileText and Cpu match; pill≠Stethoscope, route≠Plane, package≠Backpack. Verification must reconcile the icon set.

Board uses lucide 'timer' for both OPEN and CLOSING, 'x' for closed, 'ban' for blocked, 'check' for done. Brief §8/§9 specify Clock (closing), XCircle (closed), AlertTriangle (blocked). timer≠Clock, x≠XCircle, ban≠AlertTriangle. Also brief says 'open' should show NO pill (absence=healthy, open decision #4); board renders an explicit 'OPEN · T-{n}d' pill — a product-decision drift.

Brief open-decision #4 leans toward showing nothing for open windows; this board shows an explicit muted 'OPEN · T-18d/T-15d/T-9d' pill on 3 cards. Downstream must decide whether OPEN pill is canonical.

Header is a bare flex row (Wordmark | Sync) inside the page's 24/20 padding — no sticky bg-muted bar, no border-bottom, no ~61px height. Diverges from AppHeader/MobileHeader canonical chrome (brief §11 app shell sticky header).

One $card flattens MissionSummaryCard + MissionDetailHeader + 4x StatTile + ProgressBar. StatTiles are bare frames (no tile surface), ProgressBar segments are hardcoded widths (106px/80px), progress track frame has no explicit card-elevated fill. Verification must split this into the canonical sub-components.

The 4 stat tiles are bare 2-text frames (val + lab), no Cmp·StatTile surface/border/padding/icon. BLOCKED value uses muted not destructive (brief §11 mission detail header says Blocked stat is destructive-colored) — color drift.

drift_candidates.element: CLOSED task uses opacity 0.55 but no strikethrough
drift_candidates.note: The 'Book early-bird Tankwa shuttle' closed card sets card opacity 0.55 and muted name, but the task name is NOT line-through (brief §9 closed treatment requires line-through text-muted-foreground). Done tasks also lack line-through though their names are muted.

Bespoke key/value line ('BLOCKED BY:' + name). No canonical component named exactly this — closest is DependencyConnector/dep-hint. Appears only on blocked cards (2). Brief §9 phrasing is '⚠ blocked by: {names}' with an AlertTriangle; board omits the warning glyph here (the ban icon is in the pill instead).

drift_candidates.element: DONE task status button + muted name but no strikethrough
drift_candidates.note: Done tasks (4) show success-filled check button and muted-foreground name, but brief §10 done state requires line-through on the name. No strikethrough rendered — consistent omission to flag.

FAB is layoutPosition absolute at x314/y1418 within a 390-wide frame (bottom-right, correct per §11.1) but no env(safe-area-inset-bottom) offset modeled; glow color is raw hex #ff6b3566 not a token-derived primary alpha.

The full board never renders an in-progress task (orange border + bg-primary/12 + filled-square status, in-progress window). The state matrix is therefore incomplete for status; spec/verification must add it.

Group headers use an explicit fill_container 1px-high 'spacer' frame to push the count right, rather than justifyContent space-between (which the Mission Summary header DOES use). Inconsistent layout idiom across the same board — normalize in spec.

split_plan.label: Agent A — Category Board mobile layout + chrome
split_plan.scope: Spec the mobile Category Board screen shell at ~390px: page frame (bg-background, vertical, gap 24, padding 24/20/40/20), the flattened Mobile Header (Wordmark OPS/BOARD + SyncStatus), the Mission Summary card (name, TARGET line, 4 inline StatTiles with their colors, 3-segment ProgressBar), the 5 CategoryGroupHeaders (dot+label+count, sort order Medical/Bureaucratic/Travel/Gear/Tech), and the Voice FAB idle state (absolute bottom-right, glow). Document every drift on these: fused MissionSummary, bare StatTiles, header-without-chrome, spacer-frame idiom, FAB hardcoded position/glow. Map to AppHeader/MobileHeader, SyncStatus, MissionSummaryCard, Cmp·StatTile, Cmp·ProgressBar, CategoryGroupHeader, VoiceFAB.

split_plan.label: Agent B — TaskCard component + full state matrix
split_plan.scope: Spec the TaskCard organism and its sub-components exhaustively across all 13 instances: StatusCycleButton (4 variants present + the MISSING in-progress variant), CategoryTag (bare dot+icon+label drift vs /12 Badge), WindowStatePill (OPEN/CLOSING/DONE/BLOCKED/WINDOW CLOSED variants, icon-set drift vs brief §8/§9), BlockedBy sub-row, and card-level state treatments (opacity, missing line-through, missing border-l-2). Reconcile category icon mismatches (pill/route/package vs Stethoscope/Plane/Backpack), the OPEN-pill product decision, and the absent in-progress status. Map to TaskCard, StatusCycleButton, CategoryTag, WindowStatePill, StatusBadge, StatusDot.

boardTriage.notes:
PRIORITY P1: per the task's own priority guidance, mobile screens are explicitly P1 (P0 is the desktop view system + voice/toast flow + models + shared kit). This board is the mobile single-column rendition of the P0 Category view, so it is downstream of the canonical desktop specs, hence P1.

COMPLEXITY medium: the board is a SINGLE rendered screen (only the Category Board; despite the plural 'Mobile screens' name, no Timeline/Dependencies/voice-recording/toast mobile renditions are laid out here). But it is dense — 231 nodes, 13 task cards exercising the near-complete window-state x status matrix (open/closing/closed/blocked + not-started/done), a fused mission summary, and 5 category groups. Distinct-surface count is ~9 with one heavily-repeated, multi-variant organism (TaskCard). That is more than 'low' (one simple surface) but well short of 'high' (multiple independent screens) — hence medium, and the typical medium fan-out of 1-2 agents resolves to 2 because the TaskCard state matrix + drift reconciliation is substantial enough to own its own agent separate from the screen shell.

FAN-OUT = 2: split by (A) screen shell + chrome + mission summary + category headers + FAB, and (B) the TaskCard organism and its full state/variant matrix + drift reconciliation. The two are cleanly separable (shell vs repeated row) and roughly balanced in effort.

KEY DOWNSTREAM FLAGS for bidirectional verification: (1) icon set diverges from brief §4/§8/§9 on this board (pill/route/package/timer/x/ban vs Stethoscope/Plane/Backpack/Clock/XCircle/AlertTriangle); (2) CategoryTag is rendered bare, not as a /12 Badge; (3) the in-progress status variant is absent everywhere; (4) done/closed tasks lack the brief-required line-through; (5) an explicit OPEN pill is shown despite open-decision #4 leaning toward absence=healthy; (6) the Mission Summary fuses 3 canonical components and the StatTiles/ProgressBar are flattened with hardcoded values; (7) BLOCKED stat is muted not destructive. These must reconcile against the desktop boards and the canonical reusable components."

surfaces.name: StatesSheet (gallery container)
surfaces.purpose: A single 1320x900 specimen sheet that lays out the three non-populated system states of the board side-by-side as a documentation/reference gallery (mono SheetHeader 'SYSTEM STATES — empty · loading · error' over a 3-column equal-width Row). Not a runtime screen; a design catalog of states.

surfaces.name: Empty state panel (W EMPTY → EmptyState)
surfaces.purpose: Voice-first empty state shown when there are no missions. Centered, bordered card on $background with a 60px lucide 'hexagon' icon, mono headline 'NO MISSIONS YET', and a voice-prompt line composed of 'say' + the orange/primary quoted command '“create a mission”'. Carries the prototype hexagon motif and the brief's voice-first copy rule.

surfaces.name: Loading state panel (W LOADING → LoadingState)
surfaces.purpose: Skeleton placeholder for the board list while data loads. Bordered card on $background containing two header skeleton bars (skTitle 190x18 on $card-elevated, skSub 120x12 on $card), a spacer, then three identical skeleton task-card rows (66px each: 18px box + two stacked line bars 240x12 / 130x10). Mirrors the brief's 'skeletons for cards' loading state (board-list variant, no sidebar).

surfaces.name: Error state panel (W ERROR → ErrorState)
surfaces.purpose: Error-boundary fallback shown when the board fails to render/load. Centered, bordered card on $background with a 48px destructive 'triangle-alert' icon, mono headline 'SOMETHING BROKE', a DM Sans body sentence ('An unexpected error occurred while loading the board.'), and a single primary (orange) RETRY button (rotate-cw icon + 'RETRY' label).

components_used.notes: W EMPTY panel. Purpose-matches the canonical EmptyState (hexagon + mono 'NO MISSIONS YET' + voice-first prompt) but is flattened/divergent — see drift.

Three skeleton task-card rows (skCard1/2/3) inside LoadingState match the canonical Skeleton's purpose (box + stacked bars), but are hand-built frames, not refs, and structurally differ — see drift. Plus 2 loose header skeleton bars (skTitle/skSub) not corresponding to any canonical primitive.

W ERROR panel is the board's error fallback. Purpose-matches ErrorBoundaryFallback (triangle-alert + 'Something broke' + recovery action) but heavily simplified — see drift. Does NOT match ErrorStateCard (that is the search/voice 'no results' inline card).
The RETRY affordance in the error panel is a primary button (icon + label) but is an inline frame, not a Button ref.
Mono uppercase tracked labels: SheetHeader 'SYSTEM STATES' plus the three column captions 'EMPTY' / 'LOADING' / 'ERROR'. Purpose-match Eyebrow/Label·Eyebrow; rendered as raw text nodes, not refs.

drift_candidates.note: Board uses a single 60x60 lucide 'hexagon' icon (fill $muted-foreground-subtle). Canonical EmptyState builds the hex as a 60x60 'Hex' frame containing a 6-sided polygon (stroke 2px $muted-foreground-subtle) PLUS an inner 22x22 lucide 'mic' icon centered at (19,19). Board drops the polygon + inner Mic glyph entirely. Reconcile: which hex treatment is canonical (filled glyph vs outlined polygon + mic).
Board EmptyState: fill $background, gap 18, padding 24, justify+align center. Canonical: fill $card, gap 16, padding [48,24]. Surface color (background vs card) and vertical padding differ.
Board 'Msg' = fill $muted-foreground, fontSize 14, letterSpacing 1.5. Canonical 'Msg' = fill $foreground, fontSize 13, letterSpacing 1.5. Color and size diverge.

Board renders the prompt as a horizontal 'Say' frame of two text nodes: 'say' ($muted-foreground-subtle, normal) + '“create a mission”' ($primary, 600), both JetBrains Mono 13. Canonical renders one 'Hint' text node: 'Say “create a mission” to get started.' in DM Sans 13 $muted-foreground (single string, body font, no primary highlight). Different node tree, different font, different emphasis.

drift_candidates.element: LoadingState — not using canonical Skeleton refs
drift_candidates.note: Canonical LoadingScreen composes three ref instances of the reusable Skeleton (HLa60). Board's LoadingState rebuilds three skeleton rows inline as bespoke frames (skCard1/2/3), each as frame box + 'lines' frame with l1/l2. No refs used; fully flattened.

Board skeleton bars are 'frame' nodes (box 18x18 $card-elevated; l1 240x12; l2 130x10 $card-elevated). Canonical Skeleton uses 'rectangle' primitives (Box 18x18; Bar fill_container x14; plus a 'Tags' row of two bars 70x12 / 90x12). Board has fixed-width l1/l2 vs canonical fill_container Bar + Tags pair; board omits the Tags sub-row; primitive type differs (frame vs rectangle).

drift_candidates.element: LoadingState — header skeleton bars have no canonical primitive
drift_candidates.note: skTitle (190x18 $card-elevated) and skSub (120x12 $card) are loose skeleton bars at the top of the loading card with no matching reusable component. Canonical LoadingScreen places skTitle/skLead inside a Main column alongside a Sidebar of skNav bars. Board's loading variant is list-only (no sidebar at all) — a structural divergence from LoadingScreen.

drift_candidates.element: ErrorState — vs canonical ErrorBoundaryFallback (missing accent bar)
drift_candidates.note: Canonical ErrorBoundaryFallback opens with a 2px $destructive 'Accent' bar across the top and a $destructive container stroke. Board ErrorState uses a plain $border stroke and no accent bar.

Canonical includes a mono 'Code' line 'ERR · COMPONENT TREE CRASHED' ($destructive, 11/700). Board omits it; board jumps straight to the 'SOMETHING BROKE' headline (mono 15/700 $foreground vs canonical DM Sans 20/700 $foreground 'Something broke').
Canonical has a 'Trace' line ('TRACE 0x3F·a1b2c3 · build 2026.06.03 · render@TaskBoard', mono 10 $muted-foreground-subtle). Board has no trace/diagnostic line.

drift_candidates.element: ErrorState — action divergence (single RETRY vs Reload+Report)
drift_candidates.note: Canonical 'Actions' = two controls: a Button ref (Reload, descendant content 'RELOAD') + a bordered 'Report' button (bug icon + 'REPORT'). Board has a single inline primary frame 'Retry' (rotate-cw icon + 'RETRY', mono 12/700, $primary fill) — no Report path, no Button ref, different label/icon/styling (filled primary vs ref + outlined ghost).

Board headline 'SOMETHING BROKE' is JetBrains Mono 15/700 caps; canonical Title 'Something broke' is DM Sans 20/700 sentence-case. Body copy also differs ('An unexpected error occurred while loading the board.' vs the canonical longer 'An unexpected error stopped this view from rendering. Reload to recover, or report the issue with the trace below.').

Canonical kit has a Spinner (loader-circle + 'PROCESSING COMMAND…'). This board's loading representation is skeleton-only; no Spinner instance appears. Verification should confirm the loading taxonomy: skeleton for board/list load vs Spinner for voice-command processing (per brief §10) — the two are intentionally different surfaces.

drift_candidates.element: Voice-first empty copy variants not shown
drift_candidates.note: Brief §11 specifies two empty copies: 'No missions yet — say "create a mission"' AND 'No tasks yet — say "add a task"' (mission-with-no-tasks). Board only renders the no-missions variant. The second empty variant is missing from this specimen sheet.

split_plan.label: States kit spec (empty + loading + error)
split_plan.scope: Author the complete functional spec for all three state surfaces on this board in one pass, since they are low-complexity, share the same container idiom (bordered card on $background), and map to a tight cluster of canonical components. Cover: (1) EmptyState — hexagon motif decision, voice-first copy incl. the missing 'no tasks yet — add a task' variant, token/font reconciliation against canonical; (2) LoadingState — skeleton taxonomy (list/board skeleton vs canonical LoadingScreen-with-sidebar vs Spinner for voice processing), skeleton primitive normalization (rectangle vs frame, Tags sub-row, fill_container bars), composition via Skeleton refs; (3) ErrorState — reconcile against canonical ErrorBoundaryFallback (accent bar, ERR code line, trace line, Reload+Report two-action pattern, Button ref vs inline primary), define RETRY-only vs full-fallback behavior and role=alert semantics. Resolve every drift candidate into a single canonical definition per component (EmptyState, Skeleton, LoadingScreen, ErrorBoundaryFallback, Button, Spinner) and note that this board is a specimen gallery, not a routable screen.

boardTriage.notes: PRIORITY P1: The design-brief (§11 'Empty / loading / error', §12 global states matrix) and scaffolding-plan flag these states as required-for-every-surface but they are supporting/non-MVP-core chrome rather than the Category/Timeline/Dependencies/voice P0 cores — matching the task's P1 definition for empty/loading/error states. COMPLEXITY low: only 44 nodes / ~19KB, three shallow flattened panels plus a header, all using existing tokens, no interaction logic, no data binding, no domain-state matrix (no window/status variants here). It is essentially a static specimen sheet. The real downstream work is reconciliation, not authoring volume — hence a single spec agent suffices. The board is a GALLERY (StatesSheet lays out the three variants side-by-side under one SheetHeader), not a runtime screen; the spec should treat each panel as the canonical visual for that state, not as a composed page. Heaviest drift is the loading skeleton (hand-built frames, no Skeleton/LoadingScreen refs, list-only with no sidebar) and the error panel (a simplified RETRY-only card vs the richer canonical ErrorBoundaryFallback with accent bar, ERR code, trace, and Reload+Report). Note also a coverage gap: the second empty-state copy variant ('No tasks yet — say add a task') and any Spinner-based loading are absent from this sheet and must be specced in even though not drawn here.

voice recording (live waveform + REC dot + 00:09 timer)

states: job running (orange spinner, RESEARCHING…, ELAPSED 00:42, indeterminate progress bar, Step 3 of 6, NO FIXED ETA)
step log streaming with per-step states: done (check) ×3, active (spinner + source) ×1, pending (dim dot) ×3
come-back-later banner (primary-tinted, Minimize + COME BACK LATER buttons)
notes skeleton placeholder (WAITING FOR RESULTS + 3 skeleton blocks)

surfaces.name: Desktop · 03 Result & Notes (TopBar + DetailHeader + AI Notes Block + Sources + Affordances + Success Toast)
surfaces.purpose: Completed research result on desktop: the AI-authored notes appended to the task, with attribution, summary, numbered steps with inline citations, a sources list, keep/dismiss/view-sources affordances, and a success toast.
states: task detail header (category tag, window pill, StatusCycleButton, title, target date)
AI Notes Block: attribution row (AI RESEARCH · 5 NOTES · timestamp) + NEW pill, summary, 5 numbered steps each with citation chips [1]/[2]/[3]
sources list (3 source rows: domain, title, external-link)
affordances row: KEEP NOTES (primary) + DISMISS + VIEW SOURCES

surfaces.name: Desktop · 04 Errors & Edge States gallery (8 cells)
surfaces.purpose: A labeled gallery laying out 8 recovery/edge states side by side as ErrorStateCard variants, each a card with an accent stripe + icon + header + body + action buttons.
states: 01 NO RESULTS (search-x; SAY IT AGAIN + REPHRASE)
02 LOW CONFIDENCE / NEEDS CONFIRMATION (target task chip + CONFIRM + Cancel)
03 DISAMBIGUATION picker (3 picks + 'say it again' mic line)
05 RATE LIMITED (RETRY AVAILABLE IN 3:00; RETRY IN 3:00 + DISMISS)
06 TIMEOUT / TAKING LONGER THAN EXPECTED (KEEP RUNNING + COME BACK LATER)
07 PARTIAL RESULTS (2 failed source rows + RETRY FAILED SOURCES + VIEW NOTES)
08 MICROPHONE ERROR / BLOCKED (ALLOW MICROPHONE ACCESS + type-line keyboard fallback)

actions: OPEN ALL SOURCES / DISMISS / KEEP NOTES

boardTriage.notes:
PRIORITY P2: This is the AI Research / task-agent extension surface explicitly called out as P2 (peripheral/extension) in the priority guidance. It is read-only research output appended as notes — it does not implement any of the P0 MVP-core (Category/Timeline/Dependencies views, status model, shared kit). It DOES touch P0/P1 primitives at its edges (StatusCycleButton, WindowStatePill, CategoryTag, Toast on the result surface), so it must consume the shared kit, not redefine it.

COMPLEXITY very-high: 461KB/843 nodes, ~10 distinct product surfaces across two breakpoints (desktop flow of 4 large frames + mobile flow of 5), the most state-rich board in the set — it carries the full async lifecycle (record → transcribe → parse intent → disambiguate → confirm → run → minimize/come-back → result → 8 error/edge states). It introduces the largest cluster of net-new, non-canonical components (ParsedIntentPanel, ResearchJobPanel, ResearchStepRow, ComeBackLaterBanner, MinimizedJobPill, AINotesBlock, CitationChip, SourceRow, DisambiguationPicker, ErrorStateCard, ResearchAttachedBadge, VoiceQueryResultCard, ScopeChip, IntentRow) — most of the 'research/agent' slice of the 82 lives here.

DRIFT is heavy and high-value: extensive raw-hex orange tints (#ff6b3514 / #ff6b351f) violating the token-alpha rule; three structurally different DisambiguationPicker renderings; desktop-vs-mobile divergence in ErrorStateCard (accent stripe present on desktop, absent on mobile; mobile RATE LIMITED missing its action); citations rendered as chips on desktop but inline-bracket text on mobile; an indeterminate ProgressBar that diverges from the canonical 3-segment mission bar; a rounded+shadowed Minimized Pill on an otherwise zero-radius board; bespoke breadcrumb/ESC and RUNNING header badge with no canonical match; and two confirmation surfaces (Confirm Bar vs NEEDS CONFIRMATION card/toast) for overlapping intent. Two 'step' patterns (progress-log ResearchStepRow vs result note-steps) are easily conflated and must be kept distinct.

FAN-OUT = 4, split by lifecycle phase rather than file size: (A) capture→intent→confirm, (B) async running/come-back, (C) result & notes read-out, (D) the error/edge catalog. Each phase owns a coherent cluster of net-new components plus its desktop+mobile pair, so a single agent can fully spec a phase across both breakpoints and reconcile its drift. The error catalog alone (14 cards) justifies a dedicated agent.

boardTriage.notes:
PRIORITY P2: Every section here is peripheral/extension surface per the product framing — Account, Settings/Debug, Legal, Manual, and Shake-to-Report (a feedback/diagnostics utility) are all outside the MVP-core (Category/Timeline/Dependencies views, voice command + toast/confirm, window-state/status models). None of these block the read-only board. The destructive Delete flows and the Shake success/error toasts touch the confirmation pattern (P0-adjacent), but their surfaces live in account/settings scope, so the board stays P2.

COMPLEXITY very-high: This is the largest, most heterogeneous board in the set — 1.74MB / 2838 nodes bundling FIVE distinct sections (the brief said four; Shake-to-Report is a separate fifth area). I enumerated ~24 distinct surfaces/screens with ~21 distinct states, spanning two full responsive families (desktop + mobile) per section. It carries dense literal content (34 numbered legal sections, 27 list items, real legal copy) plus interactive demos and a multi-state send flow. Breadth, not just size, drives the rating.

ZERO reusable defs in this file (grep '\"reusable\": true' = 0): every structure is FLATTENED/bespoke, so drift is pervasive. The biggest reconciliation risks for bidirectional verification: (1) the Manual 'Live' demos are hand-built copies of TaskCard/StatusCycleButton/WindowStatePill and will not match canon node-for-node; (2) bespoke Switch (square 44x24) vs Cmp·Switch; (3) the Toast is much richer than canon (accent bar + reference chip + auto-dismiss caption); (4) widespread raw-hex tints (#e05a5a0d, #5ae07a1f, #ff6b351f) bypassing OKLCH tokens; (5) WindowState demo adds a BLOCKED variant that may exceed the base pill's state set.

FAN-OUT = 4, split by coherent sub-flow rather than file size: A=Account+destructive flows, B=Settings+Debug, C=Shake-to-Report (most stateful), D=Legal+Manual (most content + the highest-value drift in the live demos). Each agent owns one self-contained section family with both responsive variants, keeping shared components (TypeToConfirmField, Toast, Switch, the Live demos) within a single owner to avoid duplicated/contradictory specs.

foundations.radius: --radius = 0 confirmed (00-variables.json value 0). Board's Radius Note states 'radius = 0 everywhere' and shows a sharp 40px square (radius 0) alongside the rounded-full exceptions. cornerRadius:999 (rounded-full) appears exactly twice — the demo Pill badge and the 34px avatar — plus cornerRadius:24 once. Status/category dots are rendered as ellipse primitives (inherently round) at 14px / 10px / 7px. Brief §5 specifies status dot 6px, category dot 8px, StatusCycleButton 18px square — those exact geometries are NOT in this board (they live in the referenced library components); the board's loose demo dots (7/10/14px) are illustrative, not the spec sizes. Note line: 'rounded-full (999) only for status/category dots, avatars, pill badges & the voice FAB.'
foundations.spacing_notes: Explicit Spacing Scale block: 4px base grid, steps 4 / 8 / 12 / 16 / 20 / 24 / 32 / 48 (8 steps), each shown as an orange bar of matching width + mono px label, intro 'gaps and padding step in multiples of 4.' Observed layout gaps on the board align to this grid: section gaps 40/32/22/20/18/16/14/12/10/8/6, paddings 48 (page) / 24 / 18 / 16 / 14. Swatch chips height 52px, category chips 64px, elevation tiles 112/132/152px. Tint fills use 12% (hex suffix 1f) canonical and 18% (suffix 2e) hover-lift, with strokes at 40% (suffix 66) — matches brief §7 (12% fill / 18% hover-lift). letterSpacing values in use: 0.5 / 1 / 1.5 / 2 / 3 / 4 (eyebrows & group labels at 2, wordmark at 4, section heads at 2, big mono titles at 3). lineHeight only set explicitly on a few long-text nodes: 1.35 / 1.4 / 1.45 / 1.5.
drift_vs_brief: Type scale coverage gap: board specimens only 11 of the brief's 19 --text-* steps. MISSING specimens: brand-glyph, title-wizard (24px), title-compact (22px), subtitle-hero (18px), subtitle-dense (15px), body-long (15px), micro-xs (10px), brand-label (11px). The font-family specimens incidentally use 18px and 15px sample sizes (matching subtitle-hero/body-long) but they are unlabeled, not presented as scale steps.

StatusCycleButton DONE state color mismatch: board paints DONE with fill=$primary + glyph in $primary-foreground (orange), but brief §10 specifies DONE = border-success + bg-success + ✓ near-black (green). The board also shows IN PROGRESS glyph as ◧ (half-square) whereas brief describes ◼/filled-Square; DONE glyph ◼ on board vs ✓ in brief. Board omits the not-started→success hover and the line-through-name treatment.

WindowStatePill icons are all generic 'circle' (a dot) on the board; brief §8/§9 require distinct per-state Lucide icons: Clock (closing), XCircle (closed), AlertTriangle (blocked), Lock (not-yet). Redundant-channel rule (icon+label+color) is only partially honored in the specimen.

CategoryTag GEAR icon inconsistency: the CategoryTag spec (XDuCZ/GEAR) uses Lucide 'package', but the Colour Tokens category swatch and brief §4 specify 'backpack'. Internal board drift + brief drift.

Component count statement: DS header says 'instance the 55 registered components from the Component Library', but the canonical set provided is 82 components — the design-system board references a 55-component library subset.

Type weights/line-heights not encoded on type-scale specimen nodes: only fontSize+fontWeight+family are set; the paired --line-height/--letter-spacing sub-properties the brief marks REQUIRED are absent on the rows (present only as brief intent). Captions document weight per step (e.g. 'body · 14 · DM Sans 400').

warning token value: board hex #d9a73e matches the brief's §3 hex-mirror (≈#d9a73e derived from OKLCH oklch(0.80 0.16 80)). Confirmed consistent (no drift), but it is a prototype-new amber with no OKLCH shown on board — board is hex-only.

Tokens present in 00-variables.json but NOT swatch-documented on the board: card-foreground, popover, popover-foreground, secondary, secondary-foreground, accent, accent-foreground, destructive-foreground, success-foreground, warning-foreground, info-foreground. These are consumed (e.g. as button fills) but absent from the COLOUR TOKENS gallery, so a reader can't verify them visually. The brief's --overlay token is also absent from 00-variables.json and the board.

Wordmark size: brief maps wordmark to --text-brand-label (11px); board specimens the wordmark at 22px JetBrains Mono 700 / 4px tracking. The app-shell wordmark in the brief is 'small/mono' — board's 22px is a display-scale demonstration, a size not in the 19-step scale.
Brief open-decision #5 (keep the giant --text-brand-glyph step?) appears resolved toward DROP: no brand-glyph specimen exists on the foundations board.
foundations.notes: Board file: /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/docs/design-extract/boards/WCTkf__design-system.json (614 nodes). Tokens: /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/docs/design-extract/00-variables.json. Brief: /home/ryan/repos/Personal/ops-board/design/design-brief.md (§3 tokens, §6 type). ARCHITECTURE NOTE: this board is explicitly a FOUNDATIONS-ONLY board — it declares tokens/type/colour/elevation/spacing/tint inline, and its component sections use type:'ref' INSTANCES (not flattened copies) pointing to a separate Component Library board (refs: Button=AtbGz, IconButton=q7fK5p, Badge=SbuAZ, CategoryTag=XDuCZ, WindowStatePill=o4Fe4h, StatusBadge=PaMgT, StatusCycleButton=TK7Jz, StatusDot=xpMSV, Divider=tqj5T, Eyebrow=zteNg). Because they are refs, the canonical primitive GEOMETRIES (6px/8px dots, 18px-square SCB, 2px borders) are NOT resolvable from this file — they must be read from the Component Library board for full verification. The DRIFTS captured here are at the specimen/override level (colors, icons, glyphs applied via descendants{} overrides on the refs), which are the things bidirectional verification must reconcile against both 00-variables.json and the library defs. PRIORITY: this is P0 foundational work — every token here gates the shared component kit. The 8 missing type-scale specimens (P0/P1) and the StatusCycleButton DONE-color + WindowStatePill icon drifts (P0, core status/window models) are the highest-impact reconciliation items.
