components.structure: frame[transparent fill, cornerRadius 999 (pill), border1, padding 5/12] → L (mono 'Status' 11px/500, muted-foreground, tracking0.5).
variants: active (border/text → primary, fill → primary/12) vs default (border, muted text)
components.maps_to: Maps to a Badge/Chip pill (the rounded-full exception per design-brief §5; LOCKED #2). Reuse the adapted camp-404 Badge as an outline/pill chip variant, or a ScopeChip-style toggle chip. Label uses `--text-eyebrow`/`--text-micro` mono. P2 Manual/Legal section nav. Drift candidate: active state not represented in the extracted default (needs primary border + bg-primary/12).

components.name: CodeCallout
components.summary: Console/code callout block: left-orange-accent panel with three mono lines (comment, command, success result).
components.structure: frame[vertical, gap5, w600, bg-card-elevated, border-left 2 primary, padding16] → Ln (mono 12.5px comment '// scope, then speak', subtle) + Ln (mono command quote, foreground) + Ln (mono '→ resolves… DONE', success, weight600).
variants: line tone per role: comment(subtle) | input(foreground) | success(success) | error(destructive)
components.maps_to: Maps to the canonical CodeCallout; built on camp-404 Card/Alert surface with a left-accent border (the `border-l-2` idiom from window-state treatment). Lines use `--text-mono`. P2 Manual surface. Compose app-level: bg-card-elevated panel + border-l-2 border-primary + mono lines. No shadcn atom (Alert is closest for the bordered-callout shape).

components.name: SyncStatus
components.summary: Tiny sync indicator: 6px status dot + mono uppercase 'SYNCED' label.
components.structure: frame[horizontal, items-center, gap6] → Dot (ellipse 6×6, success) + Label (mono 'SYNCED' 10px/500, muted-foreground, tracking1.5).
variants: state: synced (success dot) | syncing (warning/animated) | offline/error (muted or destructive)
components.maps_to: Maps to canonical SyncStatus; built from StatusDot (6px rounded-full dot, the design-brief §5 6px dot) + a mono micro label (`--text-micro` 0.6875rem ≈ 10px). Reuse a StatusDot atom + Label/Mono. No direct shadcn atom; small composed badge. Note: design-brief §12 lists offline/sync as an anti-state for the board — SyncStatus is chrome (header), keep it advisory only.

components.name: AppHeader
components.summary: Desktop sticky top bar: OPS/BOARD wordmark left, actions cluster right (SyncStatus + Search/Notifications/More IconButtons).
components.structure: frame[horizontal, space-between, items-center, fill width, bg-muted, border-bottom1, padding 20/32] → Wordmark[items-center]{'OPS' primary mono 16px/700 tracking4 + 'BOARD' muted mono 16px/700 tracking4} + Actions[horizontal, items-center, gap16]{ref SyncStatus + ref IconButton(search) + ref IconButton(bell) + ref IconButton(more)}.
components.maps_to: Canonical AppHeader organism; maps to design-brief §11 app-shell sticky header (~61px, padding 20/32, bg-muted, border-bottom, z-100). Composed from Wordmark (mono `--text-brand-label` 4px tracking, orange OPS + dim BOARD), SyncStatus atom, and IconButton (ref `q7fK5p` — the adapted camp-404 IconBadge/Button). Build on camp-404 header pattern. Drift candidate (vs design-brief §11): brief says 'No + Mission button' — header here correctly omits it, but adds Search/Notifications/More IconButtons not enumerated in the brief shell; reconcile which header actions are MVP.

components.name: MobileHeader
components.summary: Mobile top bar: OPS/BOARD wordmark left, SyncStatus right; no border/padding chrome.
components.structure: frame[horizontal, space-between, items-center, fill width] → Wordmark{'OPS' primary mono 18px/700 tracking2 + 'BOARD' muted mono 18px/700 tracking2} + ref SyncStatus.
components.maps_to: Canonical MobileHeader organism (P1 mobile screens); the mobile reduction of AppHeader. Wordmark mono `--text-brand-label` (slightly larger 18px, tracking2 vs desktop tracking4) + SyncStatus atom. Build app-level. Drift candidates: (1) no bg-muted/border-bottom/padding on the mobile node vs desktop header — needs explicit mobile shell padding/safe-area; (2) wordmark tracking differs (2 vs 4) and size differs (18 vs 16) from AppHeader — reconcile wordmark token.

components.name: CategoryGroupHeader
components.summary: Category-view group header: 8px category dot + cat-colored mono label + dim done/total count.
components.structure: frame[horizontal, items-center, gap8] → Dot (ellipse 8×8, cat-medical) + Label (mono 'MEDICAL' 12px/700, cat-medical, tracking1.5) + Count (mono '2/4' 12px normal, subtle).
variants: one per category: medical/bureaucratic/travel/gear/tech (dot+label recolor via cat token)
components.maps_to: Canonical CategoryGroupHeader (P0 Category view). Maps to design-brief §11 Category view group header (8px category dot + cat-colored mono eyebrow + done/total count). Composed from a category StatusDot (8px rounded-full) + Label/Eyebrow (mono `--text-eyebrow`, recolored per category) + mono caption Count. Build app-level. Note per design-brief §4/§6 redundant-channel rule, the category should also carry its Lucide icon (Stethoscope etc.) — extracted header shows dot+label only. Drift candidate: missing category Lucide icon (color+label present, icon absent — design-brief §6 mandates icon+label+color).

components.name: ViewTabs
components.summary: Top-level view switcher: BY CATEGORY / TIMELINE / DEPENDENCIES mono tabs with an orange underline on the active tab.
components.structure: frame[horizontal, items-center, gap28, fill width, bg-muted, border-bottom1, padding 0/32] → Tab Active[border-bottom2 primary, padding14/0]{'BY CATEGORY' mono 12px/700 primary tracking1.5} + Tab[padding14/0]{'TIMELINE' mono muted} + Tab[padding14/0]{'DEPENDENCIES' mono muted}.
variants: active tab (orange text + 2px orange underline) vs inactive (muted, no underline)
components.maps_to: Canonical ViewTabs (P0 — the 3 core views switcher). Maps to camp-404 SegmentedControl / shadcn Tabs (design-brief §14 'SegmentedControl (view tabs)'). Tab labels use `--text-eyebrow`/mono 12px; active = `text-primary` + `border-b-2 border-primary`. Lift camp-404 SegmentedControl and re-skin (orange active). This is the underline-tab style rather than a filled segmented pill — design-brief §14 names it SegmentedControl; reconcile the SegmentedControl visual (pill segments) vs this underline-tab rendering. Drift candidate: underline tabs vs camp-404 SegmentedControl's enclosed/pill segments.

components.name: MissionDetailHeader
components.summary: Mission detail header: title + mono target date + 4 stat tiles (Done/Blocked/Closing/Total) + 3-segment progress bar.
components.structure: frame[vertical, gap16, fill width, bg-background, border-bottom1, padding 24/32] → Title (DM Sans 'AfrikaBurn 2026' 26px/700, foreground) + Target (mono 'TARGET: 2026-04-27' 13px/500, muted, tracking1) + Stats[horizontal, gap32]{4× ref StatTile(h2sG4m): DONE(success) / BLOCKED(destructive) / CLOSING(warning) / TOTAL(foreground)} + ref ProgressBar(fehXu, fill width).
variants: all-done (full success progress, optional COMPLETE pill)
components.maps_to: Canonical MissionDetailHeader (P0). Maps to camp-404 DetailHeader (design-brief §14 'DetailHeader' lift) composed with StatTile (ref `h2sG4m` = Cmp·StatTile) ×4 and the adapted 3-segment ProgressBar (ref `fehXu` = Cmp·ProgressBar). Title uses `--text-title`, Target uses `--text-mono`. Stat color semantics: Done=success, Blocked=destructive, Closing=warning (amber — replaces prototype 'days overdue' per design-brief §11), Total=neutral. Build by lifting DetailHeader + StatTile + ProgressBar.

components.name: Sidebar
components.summary: Mission sidebar: 280px panel with a 'MISSIONS' eyebrow header and a list of selectable mission NavCards.
components.structure: frame[vertical, w280, h560, clip, bg-muted, border-right1] → SidebarHeader[fill width, padding18]{'MISSIONS' mono eyebrow 11px/700 subtle tracking2} + MissionList[vertical, gap8, padding 0/8]{3× ref NavCard(zDpJJ): AfrikaBurn(active: bg primary/12 #ff6b351f + border primary) / Passport / Visa — each with name + 'N/M tasks' meta}.
variants: mission row active (bg-primary/12 + border-primary) vs default vs hover
props: missions [{ id, name, done, total, windowSummary }]
components.maps_to: Canonical Sidebar (P0 app shell). Maps to design-brief §11 280px sidebar (border-right, bg-muted, flex-shrink-0). Header label = Label/Eyebrow mono. Rows = the adapted camp-404 NavCard (ref `zDpJJ`, orange active state + window-summary chip). Build app-level shell + lift/adapt NavCard. Drift candidates: (1) extracted NavCards show only name + 'N/M tasks' meta — design-brief §11 mission sidebar requires a window-summary chip (T-{n}d / CLOSING / COMPLETE) alongside the count, which is absent here; (2) active fill is a raw hex `#ff6b351f` rather than a `bg-primary/12` token alpha — normalize to token alpha per §7.

components.name: TimelineWeekHeader
components.summary: Timeline week bucket header: mono date range + a pill days-until label + a filling hairline rule.
components.structure: frame[horizontal, items-center, gap12, fill width] → Range (mono 'JUN 01 – JUN 07' 11px normal, muted, tracking1.5) + DaysPill[rounded-full pill, bg-muted, border1, padding2/8]{'THIS WEEK' mono 10px normal, muted, tracking1} + Rule (rectangle, fill-container width, 1px, border color).
variants: past ('WINDOW CLOSED' — relabel from prototype '{n}d ago')
components.maps_to: Canonical TimelineWeekHeader (P0 Timeline view). Maps to design-brief §11 Timeline week header (date range + days-until label; past → 'WINDOW CLOSED', LOCKED #3 never 'overdue'). Range/DaysPill use mono `--text-micro`. DaysPill is a Badge/pill (rounded-full exception §5). Rule is a Divider (camp-404 Divider, fill-width). Build app-level; reuse Badge + Divider. Drift candidate: past-state copy must be 'WINDOW CLOSED' (the extracted node only shows the 'THIS WEEK' variant — the closed/overdue relabel must be enforced as a variant, not a one-off).

components.name: DependencyConnector
components.summary: Tiny fixed 36x38 centered glyph (lucide corner-down-right, muted-foreground-subtle) used as the visual elbow that prefixes an indented dependency task in the tree.
components.structure: frame(36x38, center/center) > icon[Glyph 18x18 corner-down-right $muted-foreground-subtle]
variants: depth/indent level (purely positional; single visual style)
could carry last-child vs mid-branch elbow variant if a real tree connector is wanted
components.maps_to: New tiny presentational atom in @opsboard/ui (no camp equivalent). Build as a styled span wrapping a lucide CornerDownRight icon; pairs with the hand-rolled cycle-safe Dependencies tree (scaffolding-plan S4). Effectively the DependencyTreeNode's leading icon-badge-lite — no Radix needed, aria-hidden decorative.

components.name: UnlinkedGroupHeader
components.summary: Horizontal mono header row marking the 'UNLINKED' group of tasks with no dependencies in the Dependencies view (unlink icon + UNLINKED label + count/sub caption).
components.structure: frame(fill, horizontal, gap8, center) > [icon unlink 13x13 $muted-foreground-subtle, text 'UNLINKED' mono11 $muted-foreground ls1.5, text '· 2 TASKS · NO DEPENDENCIES' mono11 $muted-foreground-subtle ls1]
components.maps_to: Variant/sibling of camp SectionHeader (CategoryGroupHeader family) — build as a thin group-header molecule reusing the SectionHeader/Eyebrow mono-label pattern from @opsboard/ui. Distinct from CategoryGroupHeader: no colored tone, an unlink glyph, count+'NO DEPENDENCIES' caption. Drift candidate: confirm it shares the SectionHeader primitive rather than being a one-off.

components.name: TimelineBucket
components.summary: A timeline week/bucket group: a TimelineWeekHeader (ref n4lLE) followed by a vertical stack of TaskCards (ref QfQXv) — the repeating unit of the Timeline view.
components.structure: frame(fill, vertical, gap8) > [ref n4lLE 'Header' (TimelineWeekHeader), frame 'Cards'(fill, vertical, gap6) > [ref QfQXv 'Task'(TaskCard) x2]]
variants: bucket window-state (open/closing/closed/not-yet via header + child cards)
past bucket → closed styling inherited from header
tokens_used: (none own; composes via refs — header & TaskCard carry the tokens)
components.maps_to: Composed organism in @opsboard/ui (apps/web Timeline view, scaffolding-plan S4 'Timeline re-bucket on too_late_by'). Built from TimelineWeekHeader + TaskCard + a vertical flex stack — no new shadcn primitive; it is a layout composition. Verify the embedded TaskCard ref matches canonical TaskCard (QfQXv) — drift candidate if board flattens it differently.

components.name: DependencyTreeNode
components.summary: One indented row in the Dependencies tree: a DependencyConnector (ref P5mv8T) followed by a fill-width TaskCard (ref QfQXv).
components.structure: frame(fill, horizontal, center) > [ref P5mv8T 'Connector' (DependencyConnector), ref QfQXv 'Task'(TaskCard, fill)]
variants: blocked vs unblocked (driven by child TaskCard window/status)
is-on-critical-path highlight (open design decision in brief §16.3)
components.maps_to: Composed organism in @opsboard/ui driving the hand-rolled cycle-safe indented tree (scaffolding-plan S4, @opsboard/core detectCycles/criticalPath). DependencyConnector + TaskCard composition; no shadcn primitive. Indentation handled by depth-based padding, not nested DOM, to stay cycle-safe.

components.name: SettingsRow
components.summary: A 520px carded settings row: label + description on the left, a trailing slot (here a square ON Switch) on the right — the unit of the Settings screen.
components.structure: frame(520, card, border, gap16, pad15/16, center) > [frame 'L'(vertical, gap3) > [text Label 'Push notifications' DMSans14/500 $foreground, text Desc 'Deadlines & blocked tasks' DMSans12 $muted-foreground], frame 'Trailing'(slot) > frame Switch(44x24 $primary radius2, knob ellipse 18 $primary-foreground, end-justified=ON)]
variants: trailing control type (switch / select / value text / chevron — Trailing is a slot[])
switch on/off (knob justify end/start, fill $primary vs $muted)
components.maps_to: Compose: camp Card (LIFT) as the row container + shadcn Switch (new-york) in the Trailing slot. The Switch here is a square (radius 2) flattened frame — drift candidate vs a real shadcn Switch (which is a pill). Build SettingsRow as a thin molecule (Card + label/desc column + slot); maps to camp Card + shadcn Switch/Select. P2 (Account/Settings surface).

components.name: AccountProfileSummary
components.summary: 380px elevated profile card: circular initials avatar + name/email + meta row (since-date caption + a 'PRO PLAN' tinted pill).
components.structure: frame(380, card-elevated, border, gap18, pad22, center) > [frame Avatar(56x56 $primary radius28, center) > text 'AM' mono18/700 $primary-foreground; frame PCol(vertical, gap6) > [text name 'Alex Mercer' DMSans18/700 $foreground, text email mono13 $muted-foreground, frame Meta(gap10) > [text since 'SINCE MAR 2024' mono10 $muted-foreground-subtle, frame Plan(#ff6b351f tint, $primary border, pad4/9) > text 'PRO PLAN' mono10/700 $primary]]]
components.maps_to: Compose: camp Card (card-elevated variant) + a non-shadcn initials Avatar (brief §14 says 'do not bring over Avatar' for single-user — build a minimal initials circle inline, not Radix Avatar) + the canonical Badge for the PRO PLAN pill. Drift candidate: the #ff6b351f literal should resolve to a primary/12 token like the category /12 tints. P2 (Account surface).

components.name: TypeToConfirmField
components.summary: 420px destructive-confirmation input: mono uppercase label, a primary-bordered field showing the typed value + blinking cursor, and a success helper line when the confirmation matches.
components.structure: frame(420, vertical, gap7) > [text Label 'TYPE DELETE TO CONFIRM' mono11 $muted-foreground ls1.5, frame Field(fill h42 $background $primary border, pad0/12, center) > [text Value 'DELETE' mono14 $foreground ls1, frame Cursor(2x18 $primary)], text Help 'Confirmation matches — you may proceed.' DMSans12 $success]
variants: match state: empty / partial (mismatch, help hidden or $destructive) / match ($success help, $primary border)
components.maps_to: Compose: shadcn Input (new-york, mono variant) + derived match state. Build as a controlled field wrapper; helper text colored via state ($success on match, $destructive on mismatch). Drift candidate: the static 'Cursor' frame is a design affordance — a real Input uses a native caret. Used inside DangerZone delete flow. P2 (Settings/Danger surface).

components.name: DangerZoneCard
components.summary: 520px destructive-tinted bordered card titled 'DANGER ZONE' (octagon-alert), wrapping a vertical slot of destructive action rows (DELETE HISTORY w/ outline btn, DELETE ACCOUNT w/ filled destructive btn).
components.structure: frame(520, #e05a5a0d tint, $destructive border, vertical, gap14, pad20) > [frame DH(header gap9) > [icon octagon-alert 16 $destructive, text 'DANGER ZONE' mono12/700 $destructive ls2]; frame DZBody(slot, vertical, gap10) > [Row 'DELETE HISTORY'(card,border) > {LC: history icon + label + value 'Remove past tasks…'; Btn outline 'DELETE…'}, Row 'DELETE ACCOUNT'(card,border) > {LC: trash-2 icon + label + value 'Permanently erase…'; Btn $destructive filled 'DELETE…'}]]
variants: row button tone: outline (secondary danger) vs filled $destructive (terminal danger)
props: children/actions: DangerAction[] { icon, label, description, buttonLabel, severity:'outline'|'filled', onClick }
components.maps_to: Compose: camp Card (or Alert in destructive variant) as the outer tinted container + camp Button (variant=outline and variant=destructive, 6-variant kit AtbGz) for the row actions. The inner rows mirror SettingsRow structure but with action buttons — build rows as a DangerZoneRow sub-molecule. Drift candidate: #e05a5a0d / #00000000 literals should be destructive/8 + transparent tokens. Triggers the TypeToConfirmField flow. P2 (Settings/Danger surface).

components.name: DeleteHistoryRangeOption
components.summary: 420px selectable muted row for a delete-history time range: radio + label on the left, an affected-count mono number on the right.
components.structure: frame(420, $muted, $border, gap12, pad12/14, space-between, center) > [frame L(gap11, center) > [frame Radio(18x18 $background radius9 $border-hover 2px), text 'Last 7 days' DMSans14 $foreground], text count '64' mono12 $muted-foreground]
variants: selected vs unselected (Radio filled dot / accent border $primary; row fill/border emphasis)
components.maps_to: Compose: shadcn RadioGroup/RadioGroupItem (new-york, Radio in canonical kit) inside a Card-like row. Build as a label-wrapped radio option; selected state drives border→$primary + filled dot. Member of a RadioGroup inside the delete-history dialog. Drift candidate: the Radio here is an empty 18px circle (no checked dot rendered) — confirm checked-state styling against canonical Cmp·Radio. P2.

components.name: DebugInfoRow
components.summary: 360px elevated mono key/value row for debug/diagnostics info (fixed 104px key column + fill value).
components.structure: frame(360, $card-elevated, gap12, pad9/13) > [text K 'app.version' mono11 $muted-foreground (fixed 104w), text V '2.4.1' mono11/500 $foreground (fill, lineHeight1.4)]
variants: long value wrap (value is fixed-width fill, wraps)
components.maps_to: New tiny mono KV-row atom/molecule in @opsboard/ui (debug/about panel, P2). No camp primitive; build as a 2-column flex row with mono font (--font-jetbrains-mono). Sibling to ReportComposer's KV rows — could share a KVRow primitive. Used in Settings → Debug/About. P2 (peripheral).

components.name: ErrorLogRow
components.summary: 360px elevated log entry: top line (timestamp + severity badge + error code) over a mono message — the unit of an error-log/diagnostics list.
components.structure: frame(360, $card-elevated, vertical, gap6, pad10/13) > [frame TopLine(gap8, center) > [text T '14:32:01' mono11 $muted-foreground, frame Badge(#e05a5a1f tint) > text S 'ERROR' mono9/700 $destructive ls1, text C 'OPS-4012' mono11/600 $primary], text M 'Voice transcription timed out after 8s' mono11 $foreground (fill, lineHeight1.4)]
variants: severity: ERROR / WARN / INFO (badge tone + tint: $destructive / $warning / $muted)
components.maps_to: Compose: a row + the canonical Badge (severity variants) for the severity pill. Build ErrorLogRow as a molecule reusing Badge; the code uses $primary as a mono link-style chip (could be CodeCallout/Label/Mono). Drift candidate: inline #e05a5a1f tint should be the destructive/12 token; severity badge should reuse canonical StatusBadge/Badge variants rather than a bespoke frame. P2 (Debug/error-log surface).

components.name: FeatureFlagChip
components.summary: Small success-tinted chip for an enabled feature flag: check icon + flag name in mono.
components.structure: frame(success/12 tint #5ae07a1f, transparent border, gap6, pad3/7, center) > [icon check 11x11 $success, text 'voice_v2' mono11/500 $success]
variants: enabled (check icon, $success tint) vs disabled (x icon, $muted/$destructive tint)
components.maps_to: Compose: the canonical Badge with a success tone variant. Build as a Badge wrapper (icon + label) — same family as CategoryTag/Badge. Drift candidate: the #5ae07a1f / #5ae07a00 literals should resolve to a success/12 token (Badge currently lacks a success variant — add one alongside window-state/status variants per brief §14). P2 (feature-flags/debug surface).

components.name: ReportComposer
components.summary: 400px bug-report panel: header (bug icon + 'REPORT A BUG' + close), an auto-attached-context block (KV rows + diagnostics toggle), a live voice-dictation capture block (stop btn + waveform + REC status), a description textarea, and a SEND REPORT button.
components.structure: frame(400, card, border, vertical, gap16, pad20) > [Header{TitleGroup: bug icon + 'REPORT A BUG' mono14/700; Close 32x32 border + x icon}; Context Block(card-elevated,border,vertical) > {CtxLabel: paperclip + 'AUTO-ATTACHED CONTEXT'; KV rows x3 (LAST ERROR→OPS-4012, SCREEN→Category Board, APP VERSION→1.4.2); Divider; Toggle Row{'Include diagnostics'+sub | Switch ON}}; Voice Dictation(card-elevated,$primary border,vertical) > {VLabel: mic + 'DICTATE YOUR REPORT' | Status: $destructive dot + 'REC 00:09'; Capture: Stop btn 52 $destructive circle + square icon, WfWrap{Waveform ~33 bars $primary/$muted-foreground-subtle + Hint 'TRANSCRIBING LIVE · TAP TO STOP'}}; Description Field{'DESCRIPTION' label + Box h96 $background $primary border + value text w/ ▋ caret}; Send Report btn(fill h48 $primary, send icon + 'SEND REPORT')]
variants: report type: bug vs feature-request (icon/title swap)
dictation state: idle / recording (REC + waveform active) / transcribing / done
components.maps_to: Compose: camp Card/Dialog shell + the canonical Waveform (LIFT) + shadcn Textarea (Cmp·Textarea) + Switch + Button (send, primary) + IconButton (close). The Stop button + waveform reuse the voice RecordingPanel/VoiceFAB capture pattern (scaffolding-plan S5). Drift candidate: the waveform here is a hand-laid 33-bar frame, not the canonical Waveform component — must reconcile to the lifted Waveform. The KV rows duplicate DebugInfoRow's KV pattern. Reached via ShakeReportSheet. P2 (shake-to-report extension).

components.name: ShakeReportSheet
components.summary: 330px centered shake-to-report bottom sheet: icon badge (vibrate), heading + explainer, two action buttons (REPORT A BUG primary / REQUEST A FEATURE secondary), and a dismiss footer with 'shake again' hint.
components.structure: frame(330, card, border, vertical, gap18, pad24, center) > [Icon Badge(52x52 card-elevated,border, vibrate icon $primary); Heading(vertical,gap8,center) > {Title 'Report an error?' DMSans21/600, Explainer DMSans14 $muted-foreground center}; Actions(vertical,gap10) > {Report Bug btn(fill h48 $primary, bug + 'REPORT A BUG'), Request Feature btn(fill h48 $secondary,border, lightbulb + 'REQUEST A FEATURE')}; Footer(vertical,gap6,center) > {Dismiss 'DISMISS' mono12/600, Hint '↻ SHAKE AGAIN TO DISMISS' mono10}]
components.maps_to: Compose: shadcn Dialog/Sheet (new-york) as the modal container + camp Button (primary + secondary/outline variants) for the two actions + an inline icon-badge (camp IconBadge). Opens ReportComposer on action. Brief §14 reserves Dialog for voice disambiguation in MVP, so this is an extension surface. P2 (shake-to-report). Build as a Dialog content composition; the icon badge reuses IconBadge.

components.name: DisambiguationPicker
components.summary: Fill-width voice-disambiguation card: warning header ('DISAMBIGUATION'), a prompt, a vertical list of candidate task cards (name + match %, CategoryTag + Confidence badge), and a 'SAY IT AGAIN' retry button.
components.structure: frame(fill, card, border, vertical, gap14, pad18) > [Head(gap9) > {git-fork icon $warning, 'DISAMBIGUATION' mono12/700 $warning ls1.5}; Prompt text DMSans14 $foreground; Candidates(vertical,gap10) > Candidate x3{ frame(card-elevated, border|$primary on top match) > [Top: Name DMSans15/600 + Pct mono14/700 ($success 92% / $warning 64% / $muted-foreground 41%); Meta(gap8) > [ref XDuCZ CategoryTag (BUREAUCRATIC, $cat-bureaucratic), ref SbuAZ Badge 'NN% MATCH' (Confidence)]] }; Retry btn(fill h44, border, mic icon + 'SAY IT AGAIN' $muted-foreground)]
variants: candidate confidence tone: high $success / medium $warning / low $muted-foreground
props: candidates: { taskId, name, matchPct, category, confidenceLabel, selected? }[]
components.maps_to: Compose: shadcn Dialog (the ONE MVP-sanctioned Dialog use per brief §14 — 'reserved for voice disambiguation only') + Card per candidate + canonical CategoryTag (XDuCZ) + Badge (SbuAZ as Confidence) + Button (retry, mic). Part of the P0 voice command/confirm flow (scaffolding-plan S5 disambiguation toast/dialog). Drift candidate: candidate Category ref overrides fill to #5aa0e01f (a hardcoded cat-bureaucratic/12) instead of letting CategoryTag own its tone token — reconcile to category-driven tint. Confidence badge reuses canonical Badge but content-only (no tone variant applied despite the colored Pct). P0/P1.

components.name: ResearchAttachedBadge
components.summary: Small primary-tinted chip indicating an active research job on a task: pulse dot + 'RESEARCHING' + elapsed timer.
components.structure: frame(#ff6b351f primary/12 tint, $primary border, gap6, pad4/8, center) > [ellipse Pulse 6x6 $primary, text 'RESEARCHING' mono10/700 $primary ls1, text Timer '00:42' mono10/700 $primary ls0.5]
variants: state: researching (pulse + timer) vs attached/done (static, maybe check + 'RESEARCH' label)
components.maps_to: Compose: canonical Badge (primary tone) + an animated pulse StatusDot. Note canonical kit lists a separate ResearchAttachedBadge AND a ResearchAttachedBadge name — build as a Badge with a live pulse dot. Drift candidate: #ff6b351f literal should be the primary/12 token; pulse dot should reuse the StatusDot atom. Belongs to the AI Research task-agent extension. P2 (Research surface).

components.name: VoiceQueryResultCard
components.summary: 540px voice-query answer card with a primary left-accent stripe: header ('CLOSING THIS WEEK'), a natural-language answer line, and a divided list of task name + window rows — the read-only info surface for voice 'query' intents.

usageMap.notes:
SOURCE NOTE: The requested board vsP7L (component-usage-map) is NOT a component-to-component composition graph. It is a BOARD-TO-COMPONENT AUDIT: a 4-column table (BOARD | STATUS | MAPS TO existing | NEW registered) covering the 8 product boards. Every board carries an "AD-HOC" status pill; the header states "0 / 8 boards currently instance the library — all were built before components existed, so every element is a hand-built ad-hoc frame." So it defines a MIGRATION plan (which existing components each board should instance + which new ones to register), not an atoms->molecules hierarchy. I therefore extracted the TRUE composition graph from the canonical reusable component defs (docs/design-extract/components/components-batch1-4.json), where type:"ref" edges are the actual composed_of relationships. Files: vsP7L__component-usage-map.json (audit), components-batch1-4.json (composition source), 00-variables.json (tokens).

BUILD ORDER (atoms -> molecules -> organisms), from the ref-dependency DAG:
TIER 0 (tokens): 00-variables.json (OKLCH/hex + radius=0 sharp corners). Build first; all fills reference $primary/$card/$cat-*.
TIER 1 (pure atoms, no deps): StatusDot, Divider, Eyebrow, all Type/* + Label/* typography, IconButton, Badge, CategoryTag, WindowStatePill, StatusBadge, StatusCycleButton, Cmp·StatTile, Cmp·ProgressBar, Cmp·Switch/Checkbox/Radio, Cmp·TextInput/Textarea/Select/SearchField, SyncStatus, DependencyConnector, IntentRow, CitationChip, Spinner, Button, DebugInfoRow, FeatureFlagChip, ManualTOCItem, SectionNavChip, ResearchAttachedBadge.
TIER 2 (molecules / leaf organisms): SourceRow, ResearchStepRow, Toast, Alert, EmptyState, Skeleton, ScopeChip, VoiceFAB, CategoryGroupHeader, ViewTabs, TimelineWeekHeader, UnlinkedGroupHeader, Cmp·NavCard, Cmp·Card, ComeBackLaterBanner, ErrorStateCard, MinimizedJobPill, CodeCallout, LegalSection, LegalIndexRow, SettingsRow, AccountProfileSummary, TypeToConfirmField, DeleteHistoryRangeOption, ErrorLogRow, TaskCard (leaf), MissionSummaryCard (leaf), RecordingPanel (leaf), VoiceQueryResultCard (leaf), ManualLiveBlock (slot host).
TIER 3 (organisms that ref tier-1/2): AppHeader(->SyncStatus,IconButton), MobileHeader(->SyncStatus), MissionDetailHeader(->StatTile,ProgressBar), Sidebar(->NavCard), TimelineBucket(->TimelineWeekHeader,TaskCard), DependencyTreeNode(->DependencyConnector,TaskCard), ParsedIntentPanel(->IntentRow), ResearchJobPanel(->ResearchStepRow), AINotesBlock(->CitationChip,SourceRow), DisambiguationPicker(->CategoryTag,Badge), ErrorBoundaryFallback(->Button), LoadingScreen(->Skeleton), ReportComposer/ShakeReportSheet/DangerZoneCard (leaf organisms, heavy inline drift).
CRITICAL PATH: TaskCard is the most-depended-on organism (TimelineBucket + DependencyTreeNode require it) — prioritize within P0.

DRIFT CANDIDATES (bespoke internals that duplicate a canonical atom instead of ref-ing it; bidirectional verification must reconcile):
1. TaskCard inline Cycle=StatusCycleButton, Tag=CategoryTag, Win=WindowStatePill (HIGH, core P0).
2. MissionSummaryCard inline 4 stat tiles=Cmp·StatTile; inline 3-seg progress=Cmp·ProgressBar.
3. ResearchJobPanel inline Track=Cmp·ProgressBar.
4. Button re-implemented inline in ReportComposer(Send), ShakeReportSheet(Report Bug/Request Feature), DangerZoneCard(DELETE x2), ErrorStateCard(Say it again), ErrorBoundaryFallback(Report sub-button) — only ErrorBoundaryFallback Reload is a real ref.
5. Cmp·Switch re-implemented inline in SettingsRow.Trailing + ReportComposer.
6. Cmp·Textarea re-implemented inline as ReportComposer Description box.
7. RecordingPanel waveform (~40 bars) and ReportComposer waveform (~32 bars) duplicate a waveform pattern — candidate shared Waveform atom (currently unregistered).
8. Badge duplicated inline as ParsedIntentPanel ConfidenceChip, AINotesBlock NEW pill, ErrorLogRow ERROR badge.
9. StatusDot duplicated inline in CategoryGroupHeader/SourceRow/RecordingPanel dots; Divider duplicated inline as TimelineWeekHeader Rule (soft drift).
10. AppHeader + MobileHeader Wordmark duplicated inline (no shared Wordmark atom registered).
11. Cmp·Card and TaskCard structurally near-identical (status box + name + meta-tag) = dedup candidate.
12. DeleteHistoryRangeOption inline radio=Cmp·Radio.

USAGE-MAP NEW-COMPONENT NAMES NOT in the 82-canonical list (registered-but-unverified / naming-drift, from the board's 'NEW COMPONENTS (REGISTERED)' column): NoCliffBucket (Timeline), CriticalPathAccent + UnlinkedGroup (Dependencies; canonical = UnlinkedGroupHeader), VoiceTranscriptCard (Voice; canonical kit has VoiceQueryResultCard instead), MobileSectionNav (Mobile), SettingsGroupHeader (Settings). These appear on the map but have NO canonical reusable def in batches 1-4 — flag for bidirectional reconciliation. The map's voice row also notes 'most elements already covered', implying the voice surface should be near-fully composed from existing atoms."
